subject: GRB030329: RTT150 optical observations
body:
I.Bikmaev (KSU), I. Khamitov (TUG);
N. Sakhibullin, V. Suleymanov (KSU);
R. Burenin, R. Sunyaev, D. Denissenko, M. Pavlinsky, O. Terekhov, A.
Tkachenko, M.Gilfanov (IKI);
Z. Aslan, O.Golbasi (TUG);
U. Kiziloglu, A. Alpar, A. Baykal (METU);
ïŋ―
report:
ïŋ―
We continue observations of the GRB 030329 optical afterglow (Peterson
and Price, GCN 1985) with the 1.5-m Russian-Turkish Telescope RTT150
at TUG.ïŋ― During the nights of May 3,4,7, 2003,ïŋ― series ofïŋ― 5 and 15 min
exposures with V-filter have been obtained under good photometric
conditions and average seeing of 1.3 arcsec.ïŋ― ANDOR TE 2048 x 2048 CCD has
been used as the detector.
ïŋ―
Results of OTïŋ―our photometry (based on Henden's list, GCN 2082 ), averaged
over for eachïŋ―night,ïŋ―areïŋ―as follow:
ïŋ―
ïŋ― Midtimeïŋ― Vmagïŋ―ïŋ― Verr Total Exp. sec
ïŋ―
ïŋ― 03.86UTïŋ― 21.40ïŋ― 0.07 10200
ïŋ― 04.86UTïŋ― 21.33ïŋ― 0.07 11400
ïŋ― 07.86UTïŋ― 21.60ïŋ― 0.08ïŋ―ïŋ―9300
ïŋ―

Additional note:
Probably, a moved object was found in the vicinity of the OT. This object
was labeled as "O2" in Zharikov et al. (GCN 2171) and as "A" inïŋ― Blake and
Bloom (GCN 2011).
ïŋ―
We integrated all the three nightïŋ― observations intoïŋ―one image andïŋ―
estimated the magnitudes of the sourcesïŋ― in the afterglow vicinity as
ïŋ―ïŋ―ïŋ―ïŋ―ïŋ―ïŋ―ïŋ―ïŋ―ïŋ―ïŋ―ïŋ―ïŋ―ïŋ―ïŋ―
        Vïŋ―ïŋ―ïŋ―ïŋ―ïŋ― Verr
ïŋ―
O1 (B) 23.0ïŋ―ïŋ― 0.2
O2 (A) 22.6ïŋ―ïŋ― 0.1
ïŋ―
Using the coordinates of the reference starsïŋ―ïŋ― from Henden's listïŋ― we have
determined the positions of OTïŋ― and O1, O2 as
ïŋ―
     RA(2000.0) DEC(2000.0)
ïŋ―
OT 10:44:49.958 21:31:17.50
O1 10:44:50.039 21:31:10.86
O2 10:44:49.373 21:31:15.02
ïŋ―
The comparision of coordinates with those given by Blake and Bloom has
shown that position of the source "A" is different by 6 arcsec whileïŋ― theïŋ―
theïŋ―position of the source "B" is in agreement ïŋ―withinïŋ― 0.5 arcsec 
positional error of Blake and Bloom.
ïŋ―
We estimate the color of O2 asïŋ― V-R = 0.2,ïŋ― and of O1 as V-R = 0.9
(R mag estimates are taken from Khamitov et al., GCN 2198)
ïŋ―
Taking into account theïŋ―apparent magnitudes, color and considerable 
positional shift, we suppose that this isïŋ―an object nearer to the Sun. 
Additional observations are encouraged.ïŋ―
ïŋ―
This message may be cited.
